message:
chore(beads): Sync issue tracker state

Update the local beads issue tracking database with latest status changes.
The issues.jsonl file contains the serialized issue graph with dependency
relationships, and last-touched tracks the most recent modification
timestamp for cache invalidation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-22j1","title":"Update robot-docs and help text for sources setup","description":"# Update robot-docs and help text for sources setup

## What
Update the cass documentation to cover the new `sources setup` command, including:
- Robot-docs content for LLM consumption
- CLI help text
- AGENTS.md section
- Architecture diagram
- Configuration reference
- FAQ/troubleshooting

## Why
New features need documentation for:
1. Users to discover and learn the feature
2. AI agents (via robot-docs) to understand how to use it
3. Future maintainers to understand the design

## Documentation Updates

### CLI Help Text (clap doc comments)
```rust
/// Interactive wizard to set up remote sources
/// 
/// Discovers SSH hosts from your ~/.ssh/config, checks each for existing cass
/// installations and agent session data, then guides you through selecting
/// which machines to configure for remote search.
/// 
/// The wizard can:
/// - Install cass on remotes that don't have it
/// - Run initial indexing on remotes
/// - Configure sources.toml with correct paths and mappings
/// - Sync data to your local machine
/// 
/// Examples:
///   cass sources setup                    # Interactive wizard
///   cass sources setup --hosts css,csd    # Configure specific hosts
///   cass sources setup --dry-run          # Preview without changes
///   cass sources setup --resume           # Resume interrupted setup
///   cass sources setup --non-interactive  # For scripting (uses defaults)
///   
/// For more details: cass docs sources-setup
Setup {
    /// Configure only these hosts (comma-separated SSH aliases)
    #[arg(long, value_delimiter = ',')] 
    hosts: Option<Vec<String>>,
    
    /// Preview what would be done without making changes
    #[arg(long)]
    dry_run: bool,
    
    /// Resume an interrupted setup from saved state
    #[arg(long)]
    resume: bool,
    
    /// Skip interactive prompts, use auto-detected defaults
    #[arg(long)]
    non_interactive: bool,
    
    /// Don't install cass on remotes that don't have it
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_install: bool,
    
    /// Don't run initial indexing on remotes
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_index: bool,
    
    /// Don't sync data after setup
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_sync: bool,
    
    /// Output progress as JSON (for scripting)
    #[arg(long)]
    json: bool,
}
```

### Robot-Docs (robot_docs.rs)
Add comprehensive section:
```rust
("setup", r#"
# cass sources setup - Interactive Remote Sources Wizard

## Overview
The setup wizard automates configuring cass to search across multiple machines.
It discovers your SSH hosts, checks their status, and handles installation,
indexing, and configuration automatically.

## Quick Start
```bash
# Interactive (recommended for first-time setup)
cass sources setup

# Non-interactive (for scripting)
cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css,csd,yto
```

## Workflow Phases

### Phase 1: Discovery
- Parses ~/.ssh/config to find configured hosts
- Filters out wildcards and patterns (*, ?)
- Uses SSH aliases as host identifiers

### Phase 2: Probing
- Connects to each host via SSH (parallel, with timeout)
- Checks if cass is installed (and version)
- Detects existing agent session data:
  - ~/.claude/projects (Claude Code)
  - ~/.codex/sessions (OpenAI Codex)
  - ~/.cursor (Cursor editor)
  - ~/.gemini/tmp (Gemini CLI)
- Collects system info (OS, disk space, memory)

### Phase 3: Selection
- Displays discovered hosts with status
- Shows what data was found on each host
- Marks unreachable hosts (not selectable)
- Marks already-configured hosts (grayed out)
- Supports search/filter for many hosts

### Phase 4: Installation (optional)
- Installs cass on hosts that don't have it
- Tries multiple methods:
  1. cargo binstall (fastest)
  2. cargo install (most reliable)
  3. Download binary (no cargo needed)
- Shows progress, handles failures gracefully

### Phase 5: Indexing (optional)
- Triggers `cass index` on remote hosts
- Runs in background, polls for progress
- Can skip with --skip-index

### Phase 6: Configuration
- Generates sources.toml entries
- Shows preview before saving
- Allows customization of paths/mappings
- Creates backup of existing config
- Validates TOML before writing

### Phase 7: Initial Sync (optional)
- Runs `cass sources sync --source <name>` for each new source
- Downloads session data to local machine
- Can skip with --skip-sync

## Flags Reference

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--hosts <names>` | Only configure these hosts (comma-separated SSH aliases) |
| `--dry-run` | Preview what would be done, don't make changes |
| `--resume` | Resume from saved state after interruption |
| `--non-interactive` | Skip all prompts, use auto-detected defaults |
| `--skip-install` | Don't install cass on remotes |
| `--skip-index` | Don't run remote indexing |
| `--skip-sync` | Don't sync after setup |
| `--json` | Output progress as JSON for scripting |

## State and Resume

If setup is interrupted (Ctrl+C, connection lost), state is saved to:
`~/.config/cass/setup_state.json`

Resume with:
```bash
cass sources setup --resume
```

State includes:
- Hosts probed
- Hosts selected
- Installation progress
- Indexing progress

## Non-Interactive Usage

For automation/scripting:
```bash
# Configure specific hosts with all defaults
cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css,csd

# Skip install and index, just configure
cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css --skip-install --skip-index

# JSON output for parsing
cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css --json
```

## Generated Configuration

The wizard generates sources.toml entries like:
```toml
[[sources]]
name = "css"
type = "ssh"
host = "css"
paths = [
    "~/.claude/projects",
    "~/.codex/sessions",
]
sync_schedule = "manual"

[[sources.path_mappings]]
from = "/data/projects"
to = "/Users/username/projects"
```

## Troubleshooting

### "Host unreachable"
- Verify SSH config: `ssh <host>` manually
- Check if host is up: `ping <hostname>`
- Ensure SSH key is loaded: `ssh-add -l`

### "Permission denied"
- Add SSH key to agent: `ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa`
- Check authorized_keys on remote

### "cargo not found"
- Install Rust on remote: `curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh`
- Or use --skip-install and install manually

### "Compilation failed"
- Check disk space on remote
- Ensure build dependencies: `apt install build-essential` (Debian/Ubuntu)
- Try downloading binary instead

### "Index taking too long"
- Large session histories can take minutes
- Check progress: `ssh <host> 'cass index --status'`
- Index runs in background; setup continues

## See Also
- `cass sources list` - List configured sources
- `cass sources sync` - Sync data from sources
- `cass sources discover` - Just discover hosts (no setup)
"#)
```

### AGENTS.md Update
Add detailed section under "cass — Coding Agent Session Search":
```markdown
### Multi-Machine Search Setup

cass can search across agent sessions from multiple machines. Use the interactive
setup wizard for the easiest configuration:

```bash
cass sources setup
```

#### What the wizard does:
1. **Discovers** SSH hosts from your ~/.ssh/config
2. **Probes** each host to check for:
   - Existing cass installation
   - Agent session data (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Gemini)
   - System resources (disk, memory)
3. **Lets you select** which hosts to configure
4. **Installs cass** on remotes if needed
5. **Indexes** existing sessions on remotes
6. **Configures** sources.toml with correct paths
7. **Syncs** data to your local machine

#### For scripting (non-interactive):
```bash
cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css,csd,yto
```

#### After setup:
```bash
# Search across all sources
cass search "database migration"

# Sync latest data
cass sources sync --all

# List configured sources
cass sources list
```

#### Manual configuration:
If you prefer manual setup, edit `~/.config/cass/sources.toml`:
```toml
[[sources]]
name = "my-server"
type = "ssh"
host = "user@server.example.com"
paths = ["~/.claude/projects"]

[[sources.path_mappings]]
from = "/home/user/projects"
to = "/Users/me/projects"
```
```

### Configuration Reference

Add to robot-docs or separate config reference:

```markdown
## sources.toml Reference

### Source Definition

```toml
[[sources]]
name = "server-name"      # Required: Unique identifier
type = "ssh"              # Required: Currently only "ssh" supported
host = "user@host"        # SSH target (can be alias from ~/.ssh/config)
paths = [                 # Directories to sync
    "~/.claude/projects",
    "~/.codex/sessions",
]
sync_schedule = "manual"  # "manual" | "hourly" | "daily" (future)
platform = "linux"        # Optional: "linux" | "macos" | "windows"
```

### Path Mappings

Path mappings rewrite workspace paths from remote conventions to local:

```toml
[[sources.path_mappings]]
from = "/data/projects"           # Remote path prefix
to = "/Users/username/projects"   # Local path prefix
```

**Why needed?** Sessions contain file paths like `/data/projects/myapp/src/main.rs`.
Mappings let cass translate these to local equivalents for navigation.

**Common patterns:**
- `/data/projects` → `~/projects` (server convention)
- `/home/ubuntu` → `~` (different usernames)
- `/root` → `~` (root vs user)

### Sync Schedule (Future)

Currently only `manual` is supported. Future options:
- `hourly`: Auto-sync every hour
- `daily`: Auto-sync once per day
- `on_connect`: Sync when SSH host becomes reachable
```

### FAQ Section
```markdown
## FAQ

**Q: Can I search remote sessions without syncing them locally?**
A: Not currently. cass syncs data locally for fast, offline search.
   Future versions may support direct remote querying.

**Q: How much disk space do synced sessions use?**
A: Varies by usage. A typical month of sessions is ~50-200MB per machine.
   Use `du -sh ~/.local/share/cass/sources/` to check.

**Q: Can I sync from machines without SSH config entries?**
A: Yes. After wizard setup, manually add to sources.toml:
   ```toml
   [[sources]]
   name = "adhoc-server"
   type = "ssh"
   host = "user@192.168.1.100"
   ```

**Q: Do I need cass installed on remote machines?**
A: For indexing: yes. For sync-only: no, rsync is sufficient.
   Without remote cass, you sync raw files but can't search remotely.

**Q: Can I exclude certain paths or patterns?**
A: Not yet. Currently all sessions in configured paths are synced.
   This is a planned feature.

**Q: How do I remove a source?**
A: Delete the `[[sources]]` block from sources.toml.
   Optionally delete synced data: `rm -rf ~/.local/share/cass/sources/<name>`
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-25kf","title":"[Task] Opt 7.2: Add SQLite caching equivalence tests","description":"# Task: Add SQLite Caching Equivalence Tests

## Objective

From PLAN Section 8.7:
> **Oracle**: Compare DB row counts and key sets after indexing same corpus with/without caching.

## Test Strategy

### 1. ID Equivalence Test
```rust
#[test]
fn sqlite_cache_same_ids() {
    let corpus = create_test_corpus();
    
    // Index without cache
    env::set_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE", "0");
    let db_path_1 = index_corpus(&corpus);
    let ids_without_cache = get_all_ids(&db_path_1);
    
    // Index with cache
    env::remove_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE");
    let db_path_2 = index_corpus(&corpus);
    let ids_with_cache = get_all_ids(&db_path_2);
    
    // Agent IDs should match
    assert_eq!(
        ids_without_cache.agents,
        ids_with_cache.agents,
        "Agent IDs differ"
    );
    
    // Workspace IDs should match
    assert_eq!(
        ids_without_cache.workspaces,
        ids_with_cache.workspaces,
        "Workspace IDs differ"
    );
}
```

### 2. Row Count Test
```rust
#[test]
fn sqlite_cache_same_row_counts() {
    let corpus = create_test_corpus();
    
    // Index without cache
    env::set_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE", "0");
    let counts_1 = index_and_count(&corpus);
    
    // Index with cache
    env::remove_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE");
    let counts_2 = index_and_count(&corpus);
    
    assert_eq!(counts_1.agents, counts_2.agents);
    assert_eq!(counts_1.workspaces, counts_2.workspaces);
    assert_eq!(counts_1.conversations, counts_2.conversations);
    assert_eq!(counts_1.messages, counts_2.messages);
}
```

### 3. Search Results Test
```rust
#[test]
fn sqlite_cache_same_search_results() {
    // After indexing with cache, search results should be identical
    let corpus = create_test_corpus();
    
    // Index with cache
    let index = index_with_cache(&corpus);
    let results_cached = search(&index, "test query");
    
    // Index without cache (fresh)
    let index2 = index_without_cache(&corpus);
    let results_uncached = search(&index2, "test query");
    
    assert_eq!(results_cached.len(), results_uncached.len());
    for (c, u) in results_cached.iter().zip(&results_uncached) {
        assert_eq!(c.message_id, u.message_id);
    }
}
```

### 4. Concurrent Indexing Test
```rust
#[test]
fn sqlite_cache_concurrent_safe() {
    // Two processes indexing simultaneously should not corrupt
    // (cache is process-local, INSERT...ON CONFLICT handles races)
    
    // This test verifies the "Concurrent Indexing" edge case
    // from the PLAN.
}
```

### 5. Rollback Test
```rust
#[test]
fn sqlite_cache_rollback() {
    env::set_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE", "0");
    // Should query database for every lookup
    let queries_without_cache = count_queries();
    
    env::remove_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE");
    // Should use cache
    let queries_with_cache = count_queries();
    
    assert!(queries_with_cache < queries_without_cache / 10,
        "Cache should reduce queries significantly");
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] ID equivalence verified
- [ ] Row counts match
- [ ] Search results identical
- [ ] Concurrent indexing safe

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-25sv","title":"Opt 1.3: LRU Metadata Cache (20-40% faster repeated access)","description":"# Optimization 1.3: LRU Metadata Cache (20-40% faster repeated access)

## Summary
Parsed metadata is re-parsed from JSON every time it's accessed, even for the
same row_id. A sharded LRU cache eliminates redundant parsing for repeated accesses,
which is common in browse/expand operations and TUI scrolling.

## Location
- **File:** src/storage/sqlite.rs
- **Lines:** After metadata parsing logic
- **Related:** Search result hydration, TUI viewer, expand command

## Current Implementation
```rust
fn get_metadata(row_id: i64) -> Result<ConversationMetadata> {
    let json_str: String = /* query from SQLite */;
    serde_json::from_str(&json_str)  // Re-parses every call
}
```

## Problem Analysis
1. **No caching:** Same row parsed multiple times in a session
2. **TUI hot path:** Scrolling repeatedly accesses same metadata
3. **Expand operations:** View->Expand->View pattern re-parses same data
4. **Search refinement:** Narrowing search re-parses overlapping results

## Proposed Solution
```rust
use parking_lot::RwLock;
use lru::LruCache;
use std::sync::Arc;
use std::num::NonZeroUsize;

/// Sharded LRU cache for metadata to reduce lock contention
pub struct MetadataCache {
    shards: [RwLock<LruCache<i64, Arc<ConversationMetadata>>>; 16],
    stats: CacheStats,
}

#[derive(Default)]
pub struct CacheStats {
    hits: AtomicU64,
    misses: AtomicU64,
    evictions: AtomicU64,
}

impl MetadataCache {
    pub fn new(capacity_per_shard: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            shards: std::array::from_fn(|_| {
                RwLock::new(LruCache::new(
                    NonZeroUsize::new(capacity_per_shard).unwrap()
                ))
            }),
            stats: CacheStats::default(),
        }
    }
    
    fn shard_index(&self, row_id: i64) -> usize {
        // Use good hash distribution
        let hash = fxhash::hash64(&row_id);
        (hash as usize) % 16
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, row_id: i64) -> Option<Arc<ConversationMetadata>> {
        let shard_idx = self.shard_index(row_id);
        let mut shard = self.shards[shard_idx].write();
        
        if let Some(cached) = shard.get(&row_id) {
            self.stats.hits.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
            Some(Arc::clone(cached))
        } else {
            self.stats.misses.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
            None
        }
    }
    
    pub fn insert(&self, row_id: i64, metadata: ConversationMetadata) -> Arc<ConversationMetadata> {
        let shard_idx = self.shard_index(row_id);
        let arc = Arc::new(metadata);
        
        let mut shard = self.shards[shard_idx].write();
        if shard.len() == shard.cap().get() {
            self.stats.evictions.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        shard.put(row_id, Arc::clone(&arc));
        
        arc
    }
    
    pub fn invalidate(&self, row_id: i64) {
        let shard_idx = self.shard_index(row_id);
        let mut shard = self.shards[shard_idx].write();
        shard.pop(&row_id);
    }
    
    pub fn invalidate_all(&self) {
        for shard in &self.shards {
            shard.write().clear();
        }
    }
    
    pub fn stats(&self) -> (u64, u64, u64) {
        (
            self.stats.hits.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
            self.stats.misses.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
            self.stats.evictions.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
        )
    }
    
    pub fn hit_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let hits = self.stats.hits.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        let misses = self.stats.misses.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        let total = hits + misses;
        if total == 0 { 0.0 } else { hits as f64 / total as f64 }
    }
}

lazy_static::lazy_static! {
    pub static ref METADATA_CACHE: MetadataCache = MetadataCache::new(256); // 256 * 16 = 4096 total
}

/// Get metadata with caching
pub fn get_metadata_cached(conn: &Connection, row_id: i64) -> Result<Arc<ConversationMetadata>> {
    // Check cache first (fast path)
    if let Some(cached) = METADATA_CACHE.get(row_id) {
        return Ok(cached);
    }
    
    // Cache miss - parse from database
    let json_str: String = conn.query_row(
        "SELECT metadata FROM conversations WHERE rowid = ?",
        [row_id],
        |row| row.get(0),
    )?;
    
    let metadata: ConversationMetadata = serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?;
    Ok(METADATA_CACHE.insert(row_id, metadata))
}
```

## Implementation Steps
1. [ ] **Add dependencies:** parking_lot, lru, fxhash to Cargo.toml
2. [ ] **Implement MetadataCache:** With sharding and stats collection
3. [ ] **Integrate with get_metadata:** Wrap existing function
4. [ ] **Add invalidation hooks:** Call invalidate on UPDATE/DELETE
5. [ ] **Add CLI stats command:** `cass cache-stats` for debugging
6. [ ] **Benchmark:** Compare TUI scroll with/without cache
7. [ ] **Tune capacity:** Based on typical working set size

## Cache Configuration
```rust
/// Cache configuration (can be made configurable via env)
pub struct CacheConfig {
    /// Total capacity across all shards (default: 4096)
    pub total_capacity: usize,
    /// Number of shards (default: 16, must be power of 2)
    pub num_shards: usize,
    /// Enable statistics collection (default: true in debug, false in release)
    pub collect_stats: bool,
}

impl Default for CacheConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            total_capacity: std::env::var("CASS_CACHE_SIZE")
                .ok()
                .and_then(|s| s.parse().ok())
                .unwrap_or(4096),
            num_shards: 16,
            collect_stats: cfg!(debug_assertions),
        }
    }
}
```

## Comprehensive Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests (tests/metadata_cache.rs)
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    fn make_metadata(id: i64) -> ConversationMetadata {
        ConversationMetadata {
            source_path: format!("/test/path/{}.jsonl", id),
            agent_type: "claude".to_string(),
            timestamp: 1704067200 + id,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_cache_hit() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(10);
        let meta = make_metadata(1);
        
        cache.insert(1, meta.clone());
        let cached = cache.get(1);
        
        assert!(cached.is_some());
        assert_eq!(cached.unwrap().source_path, meta.source_path);
        
        let (hits, misses, _) = cache.stats();
        assert_eq!(hits, 1);
        assert_eq!(misses, 0);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_cache_miss() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(10);
        let cached = cache.get(999);
        
        assert!(cached.is_none());
        
        let (hits, misses, _) = cache.stats();
        assert_eq!(hits, 0);
        assert_eq!(misses, 1);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_lru_eviction() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(2); // 2 per shard, 32 total
        
        // Insert more than capacity into same shard
        // Row IDs that hash to same shard: 0, 16, 32, 48 (assuming mod 16)
        for i in 0..50 {
            cache.insert(i * 16, make_metadata(i * 16));
        }
        
        // Early entries should be evicted
        assert!(cache.get(0).is_none(), "Entry 0 should be evicted");
        
        // Recent entries should be present
        assert!(cache.get(49 * 16).is_some(), "Entry 784 should be present");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_invalidation() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(10);
        cache.insert(1, make_metadata(1));
        
        assert!(cache.get(1).is_some());
        
        cache.invalidate(1);
        
        assert!(cache.get(1).is_none());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_invalidate_all() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(10);
        for i in 0..100 {
            cache.insert(i, make_metadata(i));
        }
        
        cache.invalidate_all();
        
        for i in 0..100 {
            assert!(cache.get(i).is_none());
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_shard_distribution() {
        let cache = MetadataCache::new(100);
        
        // Insert 1000 entries
        for i in 0..1000 {
            cache.insert(i, make_metadata(i));
        }
        
        // Verify reasonable distribution across shards
        let mut shard_counts = [0u32; 16];
        for i in 0..1000 {
            let shard = (fxhash::hash64(&i) as usize) % 16;
            if cache.shards[shard].read().contains(&i) {
                shard_counts[shard] += 1;
            }
        }
        
        // Each shard should have entries (statistical test)
        for count in shard_counts {
            assert!(count > 0, "Each shard should have some entries");
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_concurrent_access() {
        use std::sync::Arc;
        use std::thread;
        
        let cache = Arc::new(MetadataCache::new(100));
        let mut handles = vec![];
        
        // Spawn writers
        for t in 0..4 {
            let cache = Arc::clone(&cache);
            handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {
                for i in 0..250 {
                    let id = t * 250 + i;
                    cache.insert(id, make_metadata(id));
                }
            }));
        }
        
        // Spawn readers
        for _ in 0..4 {
            let cache = Arc::clone(&cache);
            handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {
                for i in 0..1000 {
                    let _ = cache.get(i);
                }
            }));
        }
        
        for handle in handles {
            handle.join().unwrap();
        }
        
        // Verify cache is consistent
        let (hits, misses, _) = cache.stats();
        assert!(hits + misses > 0);
    }
}
```

### Integration Tests (tests/cache_integration.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_cache_with_real_database() {
    let temp_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
    let db_path = temp_dir.path().join("test.db");
    
    // Create database with test data
    let conn = Connection::open(&db_path).unwrap();
    setup_schema(&conn).unwrap();
    insert_test_conversations(&conn, 100).unwrap();
    
    // Reset cache stats
    METADATA_CACHE.invalidate_all();
    
    // First pass - all misses
    for i in 1..=100 {
        let _ = get_metadata_cached(&conn, i).unwrap();
    }
    
    let (hits1, misses1, _) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    assert_eq!(misses1, 100, "First pass should be all misses");
    
    // Second pass - all hits
    for i in 1..=100 {
        let _ = get_metadata_cached(&conn, i).unwrap();
    }
    
    let (hits2, misses2, _) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    assert_eq!(hits2 - hits1, 100, "Second pass should be all hits");
    assert_eq!(misses2, misses1, "No new misses on second pass");
}

#[test]
fn test_cache_invalidation_on_update() {
    let conn = setup_test_db();
    
    // Load into cache
    let meta1 = get_metadata_cached(&conn, 1).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(meta1.agent_type, "claude");
    
    // Update database
    conn.execute(
        "UPDATE conversations SET metadata = json_set(metadata, '$.agent_type', 'codex') WHERE rowid = 1",
        [],
    ).unwrap();
    
    // Invalidate cache entry
    METADATA_CACHE.invalidate(1);
    
    // Re-fetch should get updated value
    let meta2 = get_metadata_cached(&conn, 1).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(meta2.agent_type, "codex");
}
```

### E2E Test (tests/cache_e2e.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_tui_scroll_simulation() {
    let temp_dir = setup_large_test_index(10_000);
    
    // Simulate TUI scroll behavior
    // Page through results, then scroll back
    let mut page_views = vec![];
    
    // Forward scroll
    for page in 0..50 {
        let results = search_page(&temp_dir, "test", page, 20);
        page_views.push(results);
    }
    
    // Backward scroll (re-accessing cached data)
    for page in (0..50).rev() {
        let results = search_page(&temp_dir, "test", page, 20);
        // Compare with previous view
        for (i, result) in results.iter().enumerate() {
            assert_eq!(result.metadata.source_path, page_views[page][i].metadata.source_path);
        }
    }
    
    // Check hit rate
    let hit_rate = METADATA_CACHE.hit_rate();
    println!("Cache hit rate after scroll simulation: {:.1}%", hit_rate * 100.0);
    
    // Backward scroll should have high hit rate
    assert!(hit_rate > 0.4, "Expected >40% hit rate, got {:.1}%", hit_rate * 100.0);
}

#[test]
fn test_expand_collapse_pattern() {
    // Simulates: search -> select result -> expand -> collapse -> expand same result
    let temp_dir = setup_test_index(100);
    
    let results = search(&temp_dir, "test query", 10);
    let target_id = results[0].row_id;
    
    // First expand
    let detail1 = get_full_detail(&temp_dir, target_id);
    let (hits1, _, _) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    
    // Collapse (just viewing list)
    // ...
    
    // Second expand (should hit cache)
    let detail2 = get_full_detail(&temp_dir, target_id);
    let (hits2, _, _) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    
    assert!(hits2 > hits1, "Re-expand should hit cache");
    assert_eq!(detail1.content, detail2.content);
}
```

### Benchmark (benches/cache_benchmark.rs)
```rust
fn benchmark_metadata_cache(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let temp_dir = setup_benchmark_db(1000);
    let conn = open_db(&temp_dir);
    
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("metadata_cache");
    
    // Warm cache
    for i in 1..=1000 {
        let _ = get_metadata_cached(&conn, i);
    }
    
    group.bench_function("cache_hit", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            for i in 1..=100 {
                let _ = METADATA_CACHE.get(i);
            }
        })
    });
    
    group.bench_function("cache_miss_and_parse", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            METADATA_CACHE.invalidate_all();
            for i in 1..=100 {
                let _ = get_metadata_cached(&conn, i);
            }
        })
    });
    
    group.finish();
}
```

## Logging & Observability
```rust
/// Log cache statistics periodically
pub fn log_cache_stats() {
    let (hits, misses, evictions) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    let hit_rate = METADATA_CACHE.hit_rate();
    
    tracing::info!(
        target: "cass::perf::cache",
        hits = hits,
        misses = misses,
        evictions = evictions,
        hit_rate = format!("{:.1}%", hit_rate * 100.0),
        "Metadata cache statistics"
    );
}

/// CLI command: cass cache-stats
pub fn cmd_cache_stats() {
    let (hits, misses, evictions) = METADATA_CACHE.stats();
    let hit_rate = METADATA_CACHE.hit_rate();
    
    println!("Metadata Cache Statistics:");
    println!("  Hits:      {}", hits);
    println!("  Misses:    {}", misses);
    println!("  Evictions: {}", evictions);
    println!("  Hit Rate:  {:.1}%", hit_rate * 100.0);
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 90%+ cache hit rate in TUI scrolling scenarios
- [ ] 20%+ improvement for repeated metadata access patterns
- [ ] < 10MB memory overhead at 4096 entry capacity
- [ ] Linear scaling with concurrent readers (RwLock)
- [ ] Proper invalidation on database updates
- [ ] All unit and integration tests pass

## Considerations
- **parking_lot vs std::sync:** parking_lot RwLock has better performance for short critical sections
- **Shard count:** 16 shards balance distribution vs overhead; power of 2 for fast modulo
- **Memory bounds:** 4096 entries * ~1KB avg = ~4MB, plus Arc overhead
- **Invalidation:** Must be called on UPDATE/DELETE; consider database triggers

## Related Files
- src/storage/sqlite.rs (cache integration)
- src/ui/tui.rs (primary cache consumer)
- src/lib.rs (add cache-stats command)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2bwi","title":"Phase 5: Polish & Safety","description":"# Phase 5: Polish & Safety

**Parent:** Epic: Encrypted GitHub Pages Web Export
**Section Reference:** Plan Document Section 14, Section 17

## Overview

Phase 5 adds safety guardrails, secret detection, documentation, and final polish to ensure users can't accidentally expose sensitive data.

## Key Safety Principles

1. **Defense in depth**: Multiple layers of protection
2. **Explicit acknowledgment**: Dangerous actions require typing confirmation phrases
3. **Pre-publish review**: Summary of what will be deployed shown before any deployment
4. **Secret scanning**: Automated detection of API keys, tokens, passwords

## Components (Tasks)

### P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner
Scan conversation content for potential secrets before export:
- API keys (OpenAI, GitHub, AWS, etc.)
- Passwords and tokens
- Private keys
- High-entropy strings
- User-defined patterns

### P5.1a: Redaction System
Allow users to redact detected secrets:
- Auto-replace with [REDACTED]
- Preview changes before applying
- Export profiles (private, team, public-redacted)

### P5.2: Pre-Publish Summary
Show comprehensive summary before deployment:
- Agents included (with counts)
- Workspaces included
- Time range
- Total conversations/messages
- Estimated size
- Security configuration

### P5.3: Safety Confirmations
Require explicit confirmation for dangerous operations:
- Unencrypted export: type 'I UNDERSTAND AND ACCEPT THE RISKS'
- Overwriting existing export
- Deploying to existing repository

### P5.4: Documentation
Generate and embed documentation:
- README.md for exported archive
- In-app help for web viewer
- CLI help text
- Error message guide

### P5.5: Unencrypted Export Warning
Extra safeguards for --no-encryption:
- Double confirmation required
- Blocked in robot mode without explicit flag
- Clear warnings about public visibility

### P5.6: Share Profiles
Privacy presets for different audiences:
- Private (no redaction, encryption required)
- Team (redact secrets, keep context)
- Public-redacted (aggressive redaction)

## Exit Criteria

1. All secret patterns detected
2. Pre-publish summary accurate
3. Safety confirmations functional
4. Documentation complete

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2fcl","title":"[Task] Opt 3.2: Implement parallel vector search with Rayon","description":"# Task: Implement Parallel Vector Search with Rayon

## Objective

Add parallel search capability using Rayon's `par_chunks` for near-linear speedup on multi-core systems.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add Rayon Import
Rayon is already a dependency. Just add:
```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;
```

### 2. Add Parallel Threshold Constant
```rust
/// Minimum index size to use parallel search.
/// Below this, Rayon overhead (~1-5µs/task) outweighs benefit.
const PARALLEL_THRESHOLD: usize = 10_000;

/// Chunk size for parallel iteration.
/// 1024 gives ~49 chunks for 50k vectors, good for 4-8 core systems.
const PARALLEL_CHUNK_SIZE: usize = 1024;
```

### 3. Implement search_top_k_parallel
```rust
pub fn search_top_k_parallel(
    &self,
    query_vec: &[f32],
    k: usize,
    filter: Option<&SemanticFilter>,
) -> Result<Vec<VectorSearchResult>> {
    // Skip parallelism for small indices
    if self.rows.len() < PARALLEL_THRESHOLD {
        return self.search_top_k(query_vec, k, filter);
    }

    // Parallel scan with thread-local heaps
    let results: Vec<_> = self.rows
        .par_chunks(PARALLEL_CHUNK_SIZE)
        .flat_map(|chunk| {
            let mut local_heap = BinaryHeap::with_capacity(k + 1);
            for row in chunk {
                // Filter check (uses let_chains, requires Rust 1.76+)
                if let Some(f) = filter {
                    if !f.matches(row) { continue; }
                }
                
                let score = self.dot_product_at(row.vec_offset, query_vec)
                    .unwrap_or(0.0);
                    
                local_heap.push(Reverse(ScoredEntry {
                    score,
                    message_id: row.message_id,
                    chunk_idx: row.chunk_idx,
                }));
                
                if local_heap.len() > k { local_heap.pop(); }
            }
            local_heap.into_vec()
        })
        .collect();

    // Merge thread-local results
    let mut final_heap = BinaryHeap::with_capacity(k + 1);
    for entry in results {
        final_heap.push(entry);
        if final_heap.len() > k { final_heap.pop(); }
    }

    // Convert to results with deterministic ordering
    let mut results: Vec<VectorSearchResult> = final_heap
        .into_iter()
        .map(|e| VectorSearchResult {
            message_id: e.0.message_id,
            chunk_idx: e.0.chunk_idx,
            score: e.0.score,
        })
        .collect();
    
    // Sort by score desc, then message_id asc for determinism
    results.sort_by(|a, b| {
        b.score.total_cmp(&a.score)
            .then_with(|| a.message_id.cmp(&b.message_id))
    });
    
    Ok(results)
}
```

### 4. Add Env Var Toggle
```rust
pub fn search_top_k(
    &self,
    query_vec: &[f32],
    k: usize,
    filter: Option<&SemanticFilter>,
) -> Result<Vec<VectorSearchResult>> {
    if env_disabled("CASS_PARALLEL_SEARCH") {
        self.search_top_k_sequential(query_vec, k, filter)
    } else {
        self.search_top_k_parallel(query_vec, k, filter)
    }
}
```

### 5. Rename Original to search_top_k_sequential
Keep the original implementation for fallback/comparison.

## Correctness Notes

### Why This Is Safe
1. `VectorRow` is Send+Sync (verified in Opt 3.1)
2. `dot_product_at` only reads immutable data
3. Each thread has its own heap (no sharing)
4. Merge is deterministic

### Why Results Are Identical
- Any entry in global top-k must be in some partition's local top-k
- Proof: If score S is in global top-k, at least one partition contains S, and S must be in that partition's local top-k
- Final sort ensures deterministic order for equal scores
